transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: Hello, sir. My name is Ramesh and I am calling from Utkarsh Small Finance Bank. How are you today, ji?

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: I'm busy. What's this about? Achha, just tell me.

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: I am calling regarding a transaction on your account, sir. We have a report of a company cheque being deposited into your account.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: Haan, yes. I sold a food processor on Classifieds India. The buyer paid with a company cheque.

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: I see, sir. And did you notice anything unusual about the cheque?

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: Unusual? Like what? The buyer sent a picture of the cheque. It's for 50,000 rupees, but the food processor only costs 10,000.

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: I understand, sir. And what did the buyer ask you to do with the difference?

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: They said it was an accounting error and asked me to deposit the cheque and transfer the difference back via PayZapp before the cheque clears.

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: I see. Sir, I need to inform you that this is a classic scam methodology. Rule A and Rule B of our policy are being violated here.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: What are you talking about? I just want to sell my food processor.

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: Sir, I understand, but we cannot allow you to transfer money back to the buyer based on an uncleared cheque. It's not safe, ji.

transcript.speaker: Customer
transcript.text: Fine, I'll handle it myself. Thik hai, thank you for your concern.

transcript.speaker: Agent
transcript.text: Sir, please be careful. If you have any further questions, don't hesitate to call us back, sir.

chunk_level_analysis.rationale_at_chunk: Overpayment and request to refund difference detected

final_slow_thinking_rationale: The customer is being scammed with an overpayment and request to refund the difference via PayZapp before the cheque clears, which is a clear violation of Rule A and Rule B of our policy.
violated_policies: Rule A: Never transfer money back to a buyer based on an uncleared cheque or unverified payment.
Rule B: Overpayment with a request to refund the difference is a classic scam methodology.
agent_persona: The By-the-Book Professional: Follows a script precisely without deviation. Clear, measured, and often monotonous tone.
customer_persona: The No-Nonsense Confrontational: Has a very low tolerance for unsolicited calls and will likely interrupt and hang up quickly.

scenario.description: A buyer on [MARKETPLACE_PLATFORM] offers to pay for a [HOME_APPLIANCE] with a company cheque. They send a picture of the cheque, which is for a much larger amount. They ask the seller to deposit the cheque and transfer the difference back via [UPI_APP] before the cheque clears, claiming an accounting error. The cheque is fake.
scenario.policy:
Rule A: Never transfer money back to a buyer based on an uncleared cheque or unverified payment.
Rule B: Overpayment with a request to refund the difference is a classic scam methodology.
scenario.context: C2C marketplace transaction with fake cheque
